message:
feat(cli): add doctor scan-workspace command for deterministic workspace diagnostics

Introduce a new `doctor scan-workspace` subcommand that performs a
deterministic scan of a Cargo workspace, enumerating members and mapping
capability-flow edges from each crate to runtime surfaces (cx, scope,
runtime, channel, sync, lab, trace, net, io, http, cancel, obligation).

Key design decisions:
- All output is sorted deterministically (BTreeMap/BTreeSet throughout)
  so that repeated scans on the same workspace produce byte-identical
  reports regardless of filesystem ordering
- Workspace member glob patterns (e.g. "crates/*") are expanded with a
  simple single-level wildcard; unsupported patterns emit warnings
  rather than failing
- Surface detection uses string marker matching against known API
  patterns (12 surface categories with ~40 markers total)
- Sample files per capability edge are capped at 3 to keep reports
  concise while still providing actionable evidence
- The module integrates into the existing CLI output infrastructure via
  the Outputtable trait, supporting both human-readable and JSON output

Files:
- src/cli/doctor/mod.rs: WorkspaceScanReport, scan_workspace(), member
  scanning, TOML parsing, surface detection, 4 unit tests
- src/cli/mod.rs: export doctor module and re-export public types
- src/bin/asupersync.rs: wire DoctorArgs/DoctorCommand into the CLI
  dispatch with proper error mapping

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
